title: IANA First Source Decision
description: Decide which public IANA page is the best first source for explaining what IANA does today to a non-specialist.
question: Which public IANA page is the best first source for explaining what IANA does today to a non-specialist who needs role clarity, breadth and some operational grounding?

sources.label: iana_about
sources.notes: Institutional introduction to the IANA functions and role.

sources.label: iana_performance
sources.notes: Public operational metrics and service indicators.

sources.label: iana_excellence
sources.notes: Public statement on quality and operational excellence.

decision_criteria.label: Mission Clarity
decision_criteria.description: The source should clearly explain what IANA is, who operates it and how it relates to PTI and ICANN.
evidence_terms: worldwide network free from central coordination
decision_criteria.notes: This criterion is core to the question.

decision_criteria.label: Breadth for Orientation
decision_criteria.description: The source should cover enough categories to orient a new reader quickly.
decision_criteria.notes: A first source should not be too narrow.

decision_criteria.label: Operational Evidence
decision_criteria.description: The source should surface concrete operational signals instead of staying purely descriptive.
decision_criteria.notes: Useful, but secondary to role clarity.

decision_criteria.label: Process and Quality
decision_criteria.description: The source should show whether quality, excellence or management process is documented publicly.
decision_criteria.notes: Adds nuance but should not override the onboarding role.
